items.question: CommonJS vs ES Modules — what's the practical difference?
items.answer: CommonJS uses require/module.exports and is loaded synchronously (the legacy default); ESM uses import/export, is the modern standard (enabled via "type": "module" or .mjs), and uses import.meta.url instead of __dirname. New projects generally choose ESM.

items.question: Why is Node a good fit for Gen AI backends?
items.answer: Because Gen AI work is I/O-bound (API calls, streaming), and Node's non-blocking event loop handles many concurrent requests efficiently, with npm providing official provider SDKs and easy server-side secret handling.

items.question: What breaks if you run a tight CPU loop in a Node request handler?
items.answer: It blocks the single main thread, so the event loop can't process other requests until it finishes — the whole server becomes unresponsive. Offload CPU work to workers.
